repairs: Routed INC AutoRound XPU FusedMoE layers through MoeWNA16Config to avoid unquantized-MoE OOM.
Installed Python 3.12 headers for Triton Intel launcher compilation.
Copied source-matched vLLM v1 scheduler/request files into the active venv to repair package skew.

results.name: p512_n128_default_moe_config
results.interpretation: Valid but slower than current GGUF MiniMax p512/n128 decode. vLLM warns that no B70-specific int4_w4a16 MoE config exists for E=256,N=384, so it uses default MoE config.

results.name: p64_n16_amd_moe_seed_rejected
results.config_source: E=384,N=256,device_name=AMD_Instinct_MI355_OAM,dtype=int4_w4a16.json renamed to the B70 E=256,N=384 filename
results.interpretation: The config loader used the external file, but Triton rejected the AMD-only matrix_instr_nonkdim meta parameter on XPU.

results.name: p64_n16_amd_moe_seed_stripped
results.config_source: Same AMD-derived config with matrix_instr_nonkdim removed
results.interpretation: Completed but regressed badly versus the default MoE config smoke, so this seed should not be promoted.

next_steps: Tune or supply a B70 int4_w4a16 MoE config for E=256,N=384; AMD-derived seeds were rejected or slower.
Test MiniMax QK norm fusion after the baseline is stable.
Compare XPU graph, CCL IPC, and topology settings.
Keep GGUF RPC+SYCL as the faster current MiniMax path until AutoRound exceeds it.
